_note: Catalog ingestion inbox + routing ledger for Marion's knowledge base. Drop ANY source document (Fluidseal AB catalogs, manufacturer catalogs, OEM kit/part lists, industry/technical PDFs) into knowledge/catalogs/_inbox/ — a mixture is fine. Each file is parsed and its EXTRACTED data is routed to the correct existing structure (manufacturers/, oem-parts/, anyseals.json, markets/). Raw source files are then archived under knowledge/catalogs/<source-slug>/ with their own _manifest.json. Marion does NOT read _inbox directly; it reads the routed/normalized JSON. This ledger records what each source was and where its data went.

routing_targets.seal_profiles_with_limits: knowledge/manufacturers/<name>.json  (profile + operating_limits; merges into the main catalog via manifest.json)
routing_targets.oem_part_to_fluidseal: knowledge/oem-parts/<oem>.json  (OEM part number -> Fluidseal part number + this part's own technical/dimensional detail; for OEMs like John Deere whose relationship is part->part, not profile->profile)
routing_targets.brand_profile_crossref: knowledge/anyseals.json  (OEM/brand design-code -> equivalent codes, bridged to Fluidseal parent_profiles)
routing_targets.application_market_context: knowledge/markets/  (where seals go / why; NOT verified equivalents)
routing_targets.canonical_join_layer: knowledge/parent-profiles.json  (every vendor profile -> parent_profile -> Fluidseal/SSG equivalent)

ingestion_principle: Direct reference (verified OEM->Fluidseal part maps from the live website, e.g. John Deere T108050 -> RJD-T108050) is the highest-confidence path. Where no direct reference exists, Marion reasons from the part's REQUIRED DETAILS (type, dimensions, material, operating envelope) against the profile knowledge base to propose a best-fit Fluidseal equivalent, flagged with confidence. Both paths grow as catalogs + website data are loaded. Website (fluidsealab.com / sealsonline.com) is treated as MORE CURRENT than catalogs; catalogs add technical/dimensional data and older context the website omits. When they conflict, website wins for part numbers/availability, catalog wins for technical depth — note the divergence. (NB: the John Deere convention is RJD- + the OEM reference number, established from live data; an earlier assumption of RJD-TD<number> proved wrong except for the filler-ring internal series — see john-deere.json DATA_INTEGRITY_FLAG.)

sources.label: John Deere OEM parts (live website harvest)
sources.raw_note: Sourced directly from live category/leaf pages (12 part-type categories); no raw file to archive. Re-harvest from the same URLs to refresh.
open_items: DATA_INTEGRITY_FLAG in john-deere.json: Serge estimate RJD-TD187116 vs. live RJD-T<number> convention — confirm canonical number for OEM T187116/T187117 (not currently listed on live site).
Most leaf pages not individually opened; per-part dimensional specs captured only where read. Full per-part specs are on the live leaf pages — deepen on demand or when Shopify is connected.
john-deere-kits sub-category not yet harvested (this pass covered john-deere-parts only).
